creator: davidt@yadt.co.uk
text:
On Sat, 08 Sep 2001, kutulu wrote:
> >Description:
> ircd-hybrid attempts to open the logfile after it has switched it's userid.  In the case where the logfile is in a 
> standard place, like /var/log, that is not world-writeable, this fails.  Such is the case for the BSD port.
> >How-To-Repeat:
> Install the ircd-hybrid port and check the startup messages.  Cannot open /var/log/ircd.log
> >Fix:
> Open the log file before switching userids.  (I have sent this patch to the hybrid developers but it didn't make it 
> into hybrid6 either)
> 

Since gnats-submit got missed off the CC list somewhere, this is just to
confirm this PR can be closed, as ircd hybrid should have write permission
to it's log dirs, as it needs to open new logfiles occasionaly.  Running
ircd as root (with or without the setuid/gid code) is also unsupported and
not recommended.

-- 
David Taylor
davidt@yadt.co.uk
